agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Security-critical token generation uses broken MD5 hash, is vulnerable to timing attacks, and never enforces token expiration"
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "MD5 is cryptographically broken — tokens are forgeable",
      "risk": "MD5 has known collision and preimage attacks. An attacker with any valid token can forge tokens for arbitrary partner IDs. Using naive concatenation (`payload + secretKey`) instead of HMAC also enables length-extension attacks, allowing an attacker to derive valid tokens without knowing the secret key.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "generatePartnerApiToken and validatePartnerApiToken — `crypto.createHash('md5')`",
      "reproduction": "An attacker captures one valid token and its timestamp. MD5 collision tools (e.g., HashClash) can produce a second input that yields the same hash. Length-extension attack: knowing `H(payload + secret)` and `len(secret)`, attacker computes `H(payload + secret + attacker_suffix)` without knowing `secret`.",
      "fix": "Replace with HMAC-SHA256: `crypto.createHmac('sha256', secretKey).update(payload).digest('hex')`. HMAC is the standard construction for keyed message authentication — it is immune to length-extension attacks and uses a non-broken hash.",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Secure API token generation for B2B partner integrations"
    },
    {
      "title": "Token expiration is never enforced — 30-day lifetime claim is a lie",
      "risk": "validatePartnerApiToken accepts a `timestamp` parameter but never checks whether the token has expired. Any token ever generated remains valid forever. A leaked or stolen token grants permanent access.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "validatePartnerApiToken — missing expiration check",
      "reproduction": "Generate a token today. Call validatePartnerApiToken with the original timestamp one year from now. It returns `true`.",
      "fix": "Add expiration enforcement: `const TOKEN_TTL_MS = 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; if (Date.now() - timestamp > TOKEN_TTL_MS) return false;` — place this check before the hash comparison.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Tokens are long-lived (30 days) — currently they are eternal"
    },
    {
      "title": "String equality comparison enables timing side-channel attack",
      "risk": "Using `===` to compare tokens leaks information about how many leading characters match via response-time differences. An attacker can brute-force a valid token one character at a time, reducing the search space from 2^128 to 32×16 (512) attempts.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "validatePartnerApiToken — `return token === expectedToken`",
      "reproduction": "Send repeated validation requests with tokens that share progressively more leading hex characters with the real token. Measure response times — correct prefixes take marginally longer. Over thousands of requests this is statistically exploitable.",
      "fix": "Use constant-time comparison: `return crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(token, 'hex'), Buffer.from(expectedToken, 'hex'))`. Wrap in a length check first to avoid throwing on mismatched Buffer sizes.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Secure token validation for B2B partner integrations"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No input validation on partnerId or secretKey",
      "description": "Empty string or null partnerId/secretKey will silently produce a valid-looking token. Should throw on empty/missing inputs to prevent misconfiguration from creating tokens with zero entropy.",
      "location": "generatePartnerApiToken — function entry"
    },
    {
      "title": "Token is not self-contained — timestamp must be stored/transmitted externally",
      "description": "The generator discards the timestamp used in token creation (`Date.now()` is called but not returned). The validator requires the exact timestamp. This means the caller must somehow store and retrieve the timestamp, but the API gives no way to get it. Either return `{token, timestamp}` from the generator or encode the timestamp into the token itself (e.g., `base64(timestamp:hmac)`).",
      "location": "generatePartnerApiToken — return value"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Consider using a standard token format (JWT with HS256 or opaque tokens with a server-side store) instead of a custom scheme — custom crypto constructions are high-risk surface area for B2B integrations",
    "Add a `partnerId` claim inside the token so validation doesn't rely on the caller correctly pairing token to partner",
    "Log token validation failures with partnerId for security monitoring and abuse detection"
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Any tokens generated with MD5 are permanently weak — a migration plan is needed to rotate all existing partner tokens after fixing",
    "rollback_capability": "Changing the hash algorithm invalidates all previously issued tokens. Must coordinate with partners or support both old and new validation during a migration window.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fix is a breaking change — all existing tokens become invalid when switching from MD5 to HMAC-SHA256",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging of validation failures — impossible to detect brute-force or token-stuffing attacks",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": true,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": [
        "Switching hash algorithm invalidates all existing partner tokens — requires coordinated rotation",
        "Adding expiration enforcement will retroactively expire any tokens older than 30 days"
      ]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Must not deploy current code. After fix: deploy with dual-validation (accept old MD5 tokens for a grace period while partners rotate to new HMAC tokens), then remove MD5 path."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "MD5 is broken for security use — this is settled cryptographic consensus",
      "Timing attack via string comparison is a known, documented vulnerability class",
      "Expiration is provably not enforced — no code path checks timestamp age"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [],
    "assumptions": [
      "Code is used as shown — no additional validation layer wrapping these functions",
      "Tokens are transmitted over the network where timing and forgery attacks are realistic"
    ]
  }
}

content.thinking:
Let me review this code for security and correctness issues.

Critical issues I see immediately:

1. MD5 is cryptographically broken - not suitable for security tokens
2. Timing attack on string comparison (`===` instead of `crypto.timingSafeEqual`)
3. No expiration check in validatePartnerApiToken - timestamp is passed in but never checked against current time
4. Length extension attack vulnerability - naive concatenation of payload + secretKey instead of HMAC

Let me produce the output.
